name: Figure 02
description: Figure 02 is Figure AI's first commercially deployed humanoid, designed for unstructured industrial environments. It features 16 degrees of freedom in its hands alone, enabling fine manipulation tasks previously intractable for robots. Deployed at BMW's Spartanburg, South Carolina plant in 2024, performing assembly tasks including body-in-white operations.
deploymentNotes: BMW Group partnership — multi-unit pilot at Spartanburg plant covering seat assembly, panel transfer, and parts inspection. Expanded to 3 additional BMW facilities in early 2026.

milestones.event: First units deployed at BMW Spartanburg

name: Figure 03
description: Figure 03 is Figure AI's mass-production successor to Figure 02, featuring a redesigned hand with 22 degrees of freedom and improved whole-body dexterity. The platform integrates Figure's proprietary Helix vision-language-action model, enabling end-to-end task execution from natural language commands. Production ramp commenced Q1 2026 targeting 1,000 units annually.
deploymentNotes: Initial production units allocated to BMW Group expansion and new automotive OEM contracts. Figure 03's improved payload and manipulation envelope broadens addressable use cases beyond seated assembly.

milestones.event: Figure 03 public reveal with live demo
Production tooling completed at Sunnyvale facility
First production units shipped to BMW

name: Optimus Gen 2
description: Optimus Gen 2 is Tesla's second-generation humanoid, incorporating 11 degrees of freedom in each hand with tactile sensing. The platform leverages Tesla's existing Dojo training infrastructure and FSD vision stack, giving it a distinct advantage in data-driven learning at scale. As of early 2026, Tesla reports approximately 8,000 units operating across Giga Texas and Giga Shanghai performing internal manufacturing tasks.
deploymentNotes: Exclusively deployed in Tesla's own factories; not yet available for external purchase. Elon Musk has indicated external sales could commence 2026-2027 at a price point around $20,000-$30,000.

milestones.event: Optimus Gen 2 unveiled at Tesla AI Day
Internal pilot begins at Giga Texas

name: Atlas Electric
description: Atlas Electric is Boston Dynamics' fully electric successor to the hydraulic Atlas platform, designed to test the limits of whole-body dynamic manipulation. The platform's rotational joints allow 360-degree range of motion far exceeding human anatomy, enabling novel manipulation strategies. Boston Dynamics is using Atlas Electric primarily for research into mobile manipulation, with Hyundai as the primary application partner.
deploymentNotes: Hyundai is testing Atlas Electric in automotive manufacturing settings at Hyundai Motor Group's facilities in South Korea. Not commercially available; Boston Dynamics has indicated a measured commercialization timeline.

milestones.event: Hydraulic Atlas retired; Electric Atlas unveiled

name: Digit v4
description: Digit v4 is Agility Robotics' fourth-generation bipedal humanoid, optimized for warehouse and logistics environments. Its novel bipedal locomotion architecture, inherited from the Cassie research platform, provides superior energy efficiency on flat floors compared to fully humanoid designs. Amazon has deployed Digit v4 in its fulfillment centers for tote handling and goods-to-person workflows, making it one of the few humanoids with large-scale commercial deployment.
deploymentNotes: Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund led Agility's Series B; Amazon is the primary deployment partner. Digit v4 works alongside Sequoia and Proteus AMRs in Amazon fulfillment centers, handling totes between conveyor levels.

milestones.event: Agility raises $150M Series B led by Amazon
Amazon pilot begins at Sumner, WA facility
Multi-site Amazon deployment expands to 3 facilities

name: Phoenix
description: Phoenix is Sanctuary AI's seventh-generation humanoid, designed around their Carbon AI system which uses a proprietary cognitive architecture to reason about and execute physical tasks. The hydraulic/electric hybrid actuation provides high payload capacity and force output suited to demanding industrial tasks. Sanctuary is pursuing a robotics-as-a-service model, deploying Phoenix in retail and light manufacturing settings in Canada.
deploymentNotes: Pilot deployments with Canadian Tire for retail stocking tasks and undisclosed light manufacturing partners. Sanctuary's RaaS pricing model operates on a per-robot monthly fee structure.

name: NEO Beta
description: NEO Beta is 1X Technologies' home-assistant humanoid, designed to be lightweight and safe for operation in proximity to humans. Unlike 1X's heavier EVE wheeled platform, NEO is fully bipedal and optimized for household tasks including object retrieval, light cleaning, and ambient assistance. NEO Beta is in limited home trials with early-access program participants in Scandinavia and the United States.
deploymentNotes: 1X is pursuing a slow, safety-first rollout with extensive home trial data collection before broader release. NEO Beta operates for extended periods with human supervisors present.

name: Apollo
description: Apollo is Apptronik's flagship commercial humanoid, built on actuator technology developed through a decade of NASA collaboration on Space Exploration Suits and the Valkyrie robot. The platform's modular design allows end-effector swaps for different task categories, from pallet handling to precision assembly. Following a $350M Series A led by Google in late 2024, Apptronik secured a 500-unit contract with a global automotive supplier in early 2026.
deploymentNotes: Automotive pilot with Mercedes-Benz reported in 2024; undisclosed 500-unit contract announced January 2026. Apollo's NASA-heritage actuators provide exceptional force control fidelity.

milestones.event: $350M Series A led by Google closed

name: H1
description: The Unitree H1 is the lowest-cost commercially available full-size humanoid robot at a base price of $16,000, an order of magnitude below Western competitors. The platform uses Unitree's proprietary M107 motors and achieves locomotion speeds up to 3.3 m/s, setting a bipedal speed record at launch. H1 is widely adopted in research institutions globally due to its price point and open SDK.
deploymentNotes: Available for direct purchase globally; strong adoption in university research labs across the US, Europe, and China. Unitree reports hundreds of units shipped as of 2025.

name: H1 Pro
description: The H1 Pro is Unitree's enhanced variant of the H1, adding three-fingered dexterous hands and additional arm degrees of freedom to enable manipulation tasks beyond locomotion-only applications. At approximately $35,000, the H1 Pro competes directly with mid-tier research platforms from Boston Dynamics' Spot ecosystem while offering full bipedal mobility. The Pro variant ships with Unitree's upgraded Unitree SDK 2.0 featuring improved whole-body controller APIs.
deploymentNotes: Targeted at research and light industrial proof-of-concept use cases. Several Chinese manufacturing companies have run internal pilots using H1 Pro for pick-and-place validation.

milestones.event: H1 Pro announced with dexterous hand upgrade

name: GR-1
description: The Fourier GR-1 draws on Fourier Intelligence's decade of experience building rehabilitation exoskeletons, leveraging mature actuator and motion control technology for its humanoid platform. With 40 degrees of freedom and a focus on natural gait, GR-1 is designed to operate alongside humans in healthcare, eldercare, and light industrial settings. Fourier has positioned GR-1 as an open research platform alongside its commercial ambitions.
deploymentNotes: Research editions deployed with academic partners in China, Europe, and Singapore. Commercial pilot programs in eldercare facilities in China as of 2025.

milestones.event: GR-1 unveiled at World Artificial Intelligence Conference

name: 4NE-1
description: NEURA Robotics' 4NE-1 is Europe's most advanced humanoid prototype, engineered around a 'cognitive humanoid' philosophy that prioritizes multi-modal sensing and high-level reasoning over brute-force teleoperation. The platform integrates vision, touch, and proprioception across 62 degrees of freedom, with NEURA's proprietary cognitive AI layer handling task decomposition. German engineering rigor and compliance-first design position 4NE-1 for European industrial and collaborative settings.
deploymentNotes: Advanced prototypes are operational at NEURA's Metzingen headquarters and with select automotive partners in Germany including undisclosed Tier-1 suppliers.

milestones.event: Series C: $120M from Bosch, Mercedes, Porsche Ventures
€1B Series D led by Tether; $4B valuation. Reutlingen mega-factory announced.

name: Astribot S1
description: The Astribot S1 from Stardust Intelligence is notable for its exceptional arm speed — reaching 10 m/s end-effector velocity — and its demonstrated ability to perform high-speed household manipulation tasks including cooking, folding laundry, and calligraphy. The platform targets consumer and service applications rather than heavy industry, with a design aesthetic and capability set aimed at near-term home assistant commercialization. Astribot has backing from Tencent and is headquartered in Shenzhen.
deploymentNotes: Pilot programs with hospitality and food service operators in China. Astribot is reportedly targeting a sub-$50,000 commercial price point for first production runs.

milestones.event: S1 unveiled with viral cooking demonstrations

name: CyberOne
description: Xiaomi's CyberOne is a consumer-facing humanoid prototype designed primarily as a brand showcase rather than a near-term commercial product. With 21 degrees of freedom and emotion recognition capabilities, CyberOne demonstrates Xiaomi's ambitions to enter the humanoid space leveraging its consumer electronics manufacturing infrastructure. The platform's limited payload and battery life reflect its current prototype status, with Xiaomi citing 2025-2026 as the horizon for meaningful capability updates.
deploymentNotes: CyberOne has appeared at Xiaomi product events and trade shows; no commercial deployment announced as of early 2026.

milestones.event: CyberOne unveiled at Xiaomi launch event
Improved prototype with enhanced locomotion shown

name: GR2
description: The GR2 is Fourier Intelligence's second-generation humanoid, substantially upgrading the GR-1's hand dexterity with a proprietary 12-DOF hand design and adding improved force sensing across all limbs. Fourier positioned GR2 as both a research platform and a pilot commercial product for eldercare and rehabilitation support, drawing on the company's clinical exoskeleton heritage. The platform ships with Fourier's open-source GRx robot operating framework.
deploymentNotes: GR2 is in pilot deployment at eldercare facilities in Shanghai and Guangzhou. Research editions are available globally, with academic customers in the UK, US, and Australia.

name: Tiangong
description: Tiangong is the flagship humanoid from the Beijing Humanoid Robot Innovation Center (BHRIC), a state-backed entity established to accelerate China's humanoid robotics ecosystem. The platform is notable for being the first humanoid to complete a half-marathon in 2024, demonstrating its locomotion robustness over 21.1km. Tiangong represents China's national humanoid strategy, with BHRIC coordinating supply chain development across over 60 domestic component suppliers.
deploymentNotes: Pilot deployments in state-owned manufacturing facilities in Beijing. BHRIC has announced plans to reach 1,000 units operational by end of 2025.

milestones.event: Tiangong unveiled by Beijing city government
Factory pilot deployment with state-owned enterprise

name: Kepler Forerunner
description: The Kepler Forerunner is a heavy-duty humanoid designed from the ground up for factory floor deployment, with an 35kg payload capacity and 8-hour battery life that substantially exceeds lighter-weight competitors. Kepler targets automotive stamping, welding assist, and materials handling tasks where payload and endurance matter more than dexterity. The company raised $100M in its 2024 Series A and is targeting mass production at its Jiangsu manufacturing facility.
deploymentNotes: Commercial deployment with automotive and electronics manufacturing partners in Jiangsu and Guangdong provinces. Kepler reports 200+ units ordered as of early 2026.

milestones.event: Mass production begins at Jiangsu facility

name: AgiBot A2
description: Agibot's A2 is a full-size humanoid with particular strength in teleoperation-driven data collection, an area where Agibot has invested heavily to build proprietary manipulation datasets. The platform includes Agibot's novel FingerTip Force Sensor array providing sub-Newton tactile feedback across all finger surfaces. Backed by Chinese state funds and private investors, Agibot is scaling production at its Shanghai facility targeting light manufacturing and logistics applications.
deploymentNotes: Pilot deployments with logistics operators and electronics assembly partners in the Yangtze River Delta region. Agibot is running one of China's largest teleoperation data collection programs using A2 as the primary collection platform.

milestones.event: Series A closes with state and private backing
Pilot deployments with logistics partners begin

name: Protoclone V1
description: Clone Robotics' Protoclone V1 is the most anatomically faithful humanoid ever built: 206 degrees of freedom, 1,000 fluid-powered Myofiber artificial muscles, 500 sensors, and 3D-printed polymer bones replicating human musculoskeletal structure. A 40-second video released in February 2025 showing the suspended robot making dynamic, eerily human-like movements went viral instantly, propelling Clone into mainstream consciousness. Currently pneumatic (with a planned shift to hydraulics), the platform is not yet capable of independent locomotion — it operates suspended — but its bimanual dexterity and fine manipulation represent a capability curve fundamentally different from motor-gearbox designs.
deploymentNotes: R&D stage. Protoclone V1 operates suspended, not yet self-balancing or walking. Clone publishes open documentation and accepts developer-unit reservations. Successor Clone Alpha (consumer product) is in pre-order phase.

milestones.event: Myohand prototype demonstrated publicly — viral anthropomorphic hand teleoperated via sensor glove
Full-body prototype revealed with complete musculoskeletal assembly
Protoclone V1 viral video — 1,000 Myofibers, 500 sensors, sweating cooling system; millions of views
Named 'most anatomically accurate android yet' across major robotics publications

name: Clone Alpha
description: Clone Alpha is Clone Robotics' consumer product — a limited luxury run of exactly 279 units (believed to be a Westworld reference) targeting home use at a ~$20,000 price point. The platform features 206 bones, 164 articulation points, 26 DOF per hand/wrist/elbow, four depth cameras, 70 inertial sensors, and 320 pressure sensors. It runs 'Cybernet,' Clone's proprietary visuomotor model, and ships with pre-installed skills including vacuuming, laundry, and food preparation. A 'Telekinesis' platform enables owners to teach new skills via demonstration. Clone Alpha represents the first attempt to bring musculoskeletal humanoid technology into consumer hands.
deploymentNotes: Pre-orders open 2025. Limited run of 279 units at a consumer-luxury price (comparable to limited-edition supercar per co-founder). Bay Area AI training office planned to support Cybernet model development. Target delivery 2026.

milestones.event: Clone Alpha announced — 279-unit limited consumer run, ~$20k target price
Pre-orders open with Cybernet visuomotor model details revealed

name: Unitree G1
description: The Unitree G1 is a compact, lightweight humanoid released in 2024 as a research-focused complement to the taller H1. At $16,000 base, it is tied as the world's most affordable commercial humanoid. The G1's design prioritizes dexterous hands and upper-body manipulation over payload or speed, with 43 degrees of freedom including a 7-DOF hand per arm. Its smaller form factor makes it suited for laboratory and indoor tasks, while its open SDK attracts the global research community.
deploymentNotes: Available for direct purchase globally; strong adoption in university robotics labs. Frequently deployed alongside H1 and H1 Pro in academic AI and manipulation research.

name: Galbot G1
description: The Galbot G1 is the flagship product of Beijing-based Galbot, engineered for full-body dexterous manipulation in logistics and precision assembly environments. With 47 degrees of freedom including a 12-DOF dexterous hand, the G1 is designed for complex bi-manual manipulation tasks that require coordinated whole-body motion. Galbot's data flywheel — built from large-scale teleoperation operations — gives the G1's AI system an edge in manipulation diversity. The company's RMB 2.5B Series B, led by Alibaba and Meituan, funds a major production scale-up targeting 500 units in 2026.
deploymentNotes: Pilot deployments with Alibaba logistics and electronics assembly partners in Beijing and Shenzhen. Full production ramp targeted for H2 2026.

milestones.event: RMB 2.5B Series B closed; production ramp announced

name: Sunday Home
description: Sunday's Home humanoid is designed from the ground up for consumer and care environments — lighter, quieter, and with a more approachable form factor than industrial peers. With 36 degrees of freedom and 6-hour battery life, it targets household assistance tasks including elder care, light housework, and companionship applications. Sunday Robotics has raised $200M at a $1.15B valuation (Series B, 2026) on the strength of pre-orders from Japan, South Korea, and China — three markets with both acute demographic aging and high consumer spending on home services.
deploymentNotes: Pilot programs active in Japan with elderly care facilities; South Korea consumer pre-order program open as of Q1 2026. Chinese home trial program launching mid-2026.

name: Walker X
description: UBTECH's Walker X is one of China's longest-developed humanoids, with the Walker program dating back to 2018. Walker X features 41 degrees of freedom including dexterous hands with force-torque sensing, and has been deployed in hotel lobby, retail, and exhibition contexts. UBTECH is one of the few humanoid companies that has sustained multi-year commercial deployment experience, with Walker X units operating in Bank of China branches and Hyundai showrooms.
deploymentNotes: Deployed in hotel lobbies, bank branches, and automotive showrooms across China. Walker X has appeared at the Beijing Winter Olympics and World Expo. UBTECH is developing the Tiangong platform through the Beijing Humanoid Robot Innovation Center as a separate initiative.

milestones.event: Walker X unveiled at UBTECH World AI Conference
Walker X performs at Beijing Winter Olympics
Commercial deployments in bank branches begin

name: Walker S2
description: UBTECH's Walker S2 is the latest evolution of the Walker platform, featuring autonomous battery swapping capability that enables 24/7 continuous operation in factory environments without human intervention. The S2 retains the 41 degrees of freedom of its predecessor while adding improved manipulation dexterity and a modular battery system. Demonstrated changing its own battery pack autonomously in CNET coverage.
deploymentNotes: Targeting automotive and electronics manufacturing lines. Autonomous battery swap demonstrated publicly in March 2026.

milestones.event: Autonomous battery swapping demo covered by CNET

name: Phantom MK-1
description: The Phantom MK-1 is a defense-focused humanoid robot built by San Francisco-based Foundation Robotics. Designed for military and tactical operations, it is capable of operating firearms including pistols, shotguns, and M16 rifles. As of March 2026, two units are reportedly deployed on the Ukrainian front lines, marking one of the first known active-duty deployments of humanoid robots in a combat zone. Featured in TIME magazine's 'Rise of the AI Soldiers' article.
deploymentNotes: Two units reportedly deployed on the Ukrainian front lines as of March 2026. First known combat deployment of a humanoid robot.

milestones.event: Deployed to Ukrainian front lines; TIME 'Rise of the AI Soldiers' feature

name: KAIST Humanoid v0.7
description: KAIST Humanoid v0.7 is a research platform developed by the Dynamic Robot Control & Design (DRCD) Lab at the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST). Notable for using fully in-house designed actuators rather than off-the-shelf components, giving the lab end-to-end control over the robot's dynamic performance. Demonstrated performing athletic bipedal locomotion on outdoor terrain including grass fields.
deploymentNotes: Research platform — no commercial deployment. Active in sports robotics and dynamic locomotion research.

milestones.event: Outdoor athletic locomotion demo shared publicly

name: Noble Humanoid
description: Noble Machines is building a warehouse-focused humanoid robot designed for logistics and fulfillment tasks. The robot features a rugged industrial design optimized for picking, placing, and navigating racking environments. Positively received by robotics researchers at in-person demos in early 2026.
deploymentNotes: Prototype stage — demonstrated at industry events. Targeting warehouse logistics applications.

milestones.event: In-person demos at GTC 2026; positive reception from robotics community
